articles.title: Lift Out of Service at Derker Tram Stop
articles.excerpt: The lift at Central Park tram stop towards Rochdale is currently out of service, affecting access for passengers.
articles.content_html:
A lift at Central Park tram stop, which serves passengers travelling towards Rochdale via Oldham, is currently out of service. The disruption began on Saturday, 18 July, at 2:23pm and will continue until further notice.

Customers travelling towards Oldham can access the Manchester-bound platform via lift from The Gateway and use the track crossing to reach the Oldham-bound platform. This arrangement is intended to provide step-free access during the lift's downtime.

Transport for Greater Manchester has apologised for any inconvenience caused by this service disruption. Further updates will be provided as the situation develops.

Passengers are advised to plan their journeys accordingly and seek alternative routes if necessary. More information can be found on the Transport for Greater Manchester website.
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article is for informational purposes only and does not constitute official advice.
articles.right_to_reply: Anyone directly affected may request a correction or right of reply by emailing news@rochdaledaily.co.uk.
